title: Skill promotion manifest
description: Smallest metadata a curated skills/<id>/ package needs to be gated by evals/run.sh --skill as a real promotion candidate, per the 'Promoting a real curated skill' section of policies/promotion-lifecycle.md. Stored as skills/<id>/promotion.json. This schema documents the shape; scripts/validate.py's validate_skill_promotions() and evals/lib/common.sh's validate_skill_manifest() enforce the parts a static JSON Schema can't (id matching the containing directory, entrypoint path safety, instruction_only/entrypoint mutual exclusivity).

id.description: Must match the skills/<id> directory name.

origin.description: "agent-stack-local" for an independently authored skill, or the catalog/repositories.csv source name it was adapted from. Never a new, uncatalogued upstream import.

declared_tools.description: Tools the skill's instructions call for. Matched against '# TOOL: <name>' markers in its entrypoint, when it has one.

entrypoint.description: Relative path, within the skill directory, to its existing safe-usage check script (e.g. check.sh). Must not be absolute, contain a '..' traversal segment, or resolve (including through a symlink) outside the skill directory, and the file must exist. Required unless instruction_only is true, in which case it must be omitted entirely — the two are mutually exclusive.

instruction_only.description: True when the skill is instructions-only and ships no runtime entrypoint; entrypoint must then be omitted. failure_behavior and permission_boundary are then not applicable rather than failed for missing an executable.
